Beyond the Terminal: How "Now My Tools Know Me" Changed My View of Technology.Beyond the Terminal: How "Now My Tools Know Me" Changed My View of Technology.
Greetings everyone
"Now my tools know me." This simple but powerful statement stayed with me during the University ICT Services (UIS) session on Empowering You in a Digital Campus. It reminded me that technology is no longer just about using tools. It’s about learning how to work with them wisely and allowing them to help us solve real problems. That idea became even clearer during my Week 10 internship, where every task taught me something practical about the world of server systems.
This week began with building a Linux server from the ground up. We installed the operating system, configured a static IP address, enabled secure remote access using SSH, and learned how Docker simplifies application deployment through containers. One lesson I would share with anyone starting out is this never rush the setup. A properly configured server saves hours of troubleshooting later and creates a reliable foundation for every application you build.
Learning wasn’t limited to technical work. We also organized our project ImmuniTrack using a Kanban board, held daily team discussions, and attended the UIS symposium themed "Empowering You in a Digital Campus." The discussions on Artificial Intelligence, responsible technology use, and digital transformation reminded me that technical skills alone are not enough. Communication, teamwork, ethics, and continuous learning are equally important in becoming a successful technology professional.
The week ended with testing our database, reviewing our progress, and deploying our application to the Linux server. Watching the system run successfully was exciting, but the biggest achievement wasn’t simply getting the application online it was about understanding the journey behind it. Every command, every challenge, and every discussion contributed to a deeper understanding of how modern technology works.
As a technology student, experiences like these continue to shape my career and strengthen my passion for technology and innovation. My biggest takeaway is simple .Always learn beyond the classroom, ask questions, and don’t be afraid to build things from scratch. Every project teaches something new, and every challenge brings you one step closer to becoming the technology professional you aspire to be.I continue to thank the Almighty for making everything possible.
